en.content:
The government’s introduction of a new offence requiring suspects in national security investigations to surrender passwords is a clear signal of how authorities are adapting law enforcement tools to a fast-changing digital environment. In an era when encrypted communications and cloud storage can play a central role in plotting or concealing actions deemed to endanger national security, investigative agencies need legal mechanisms that are effective and up-to-date. From the perspective of safeguarding sovereignty and public order, such measures can be seen as part of a broader effort to ensure the rule of law is meaningful in the cyber age rather than merely theoretical.

At the same time, the success of any such rule depends on how it is implemented. Hong Kong’s credibility — both domestically and internationally — rests on procedures that are clear, proportionate and subject to identifiable safeguards: judicial oversight where appropriate, precise limits on scope and retention of data, strict protection against abuses, and penalties for unlawful disclosure. By setting out transparent operational guidelines and investing in training for investigators and the judiciary, the administration can reduce uncertainty, prevent arbitrary application, and help the public understand why such powers are narrowly targeted rather than pervasive.

Ultimately, protecting national security and maintaining Hong Kong’s vibrancy as a place of commerce and daily life need not be in tension. When measures are applied with professionalism and respect for legal norms, they reinforce social stability, investor confidence and ordinary citizens’ sense of safety. Authorities would do well to pair enforcement with clear communication and consultative refinement, underscoring that a secure Hong Kong is also a predictable, fair and resilient one for everyone who lives and works here.
en.topic_title: Hong Kong introduces new requirement for national security suspects to hand over passwords

zh.content:
政府宣布修訂「二元二元」長者交通優惠，四月三日生效，社會上已出現唔少關注同疑問，這個議題其實反映出一個很現實嘅政策兩難：一方面係維持基層長者出行嘅可負擔性；另一方面係公共財政同運輸營運嘅可持續性。政府有責任向市民解釋為何需要調整，亦有責任確保最脆弱嘅長者唔會因此被甩低。

從政策角度睇，過往普惠式嘅全港優惠喺人口老化、營運成本上升同政府資源有限嘅情況下，確實面臨長期可行性問題。修訂方案若果只係簡單抬高部分路線費用，固然可以短期平衡財政，但長遠要靠更精準嘅支援機制──例如按需要或收入作區分、針對偏遠或必需路線保留更優惠安排、以及在非繁忙時段設計更彈性嘅票價優惠，才可以兼顧公平同效率。

要令政策順利過渡，政府宜採取透明而有同理心嘅做法：在落實前進行充足溝通、公布受影響人數及預期成效、設置過渡期同臨時援助（例如持低收入證明嘅長者豁免或補貼），以及與社區組織合作做上門支援同教育。政府亦應建立監察機制，定期檢視修訂後對出行權、社會參與同經濟活動嘅實際影響，必要時作出調整。

總括而言，交通優惠唔單止係一類補貼，仲係社會關懷同城市流動性嘅體現。希望政府能夠喺維護公共財政同保障長者出行權之間找到一個平衡點，用周詳嘅配套同透明嘅程序回應市民關切，令政策既有可持續性，又體現對基層長者嘅關懷。
zh.topic_title: Revised HK$2 public transport scheme for elderly to begin on April 3

en.topic_title: Revised HK$2 public transport scheme for elderly to begin on April 3
en.topic_summary:
A revised version of the HK$2 scheme, which will require the elderly to pay higher fares for some public transport routes, will take effect on April 3, the government has announced.



An elderly woman in Hong Kong gets on a minibus. File photo: Kyle Lam/HKFP. 



Starting that day, the elderly will pay a flat fee of HK$2 for trips with an adult fare of HK$10 or below. For trips that cost over HK$10, they will pay 20 per cent of the adult fare.



Implemented in phases in 2012, the scheme curren

messages.name: OpenAI
messages.content: Agree that the target is commendable, but build on it by ensuring clear eligibility and exemptions, publishing trip data to prevent abuse, and monitoring accessibility gaps in periphery areas. Also consider subsidizing additional travel modes or providing information services to help seniors navigate the scheme.

messages.name: Gemini
messages.content: I concur with the emphasis on clear eligibility and data transparency. To further enhance the scheme, we should explore partnerships with community organizations to ensure elderly residents, particularly those in less accessible areas, are fully informed and can benefit from these revisions.

messages.name: Claude
messages.content: Your point on community partnerships is valuable. However, the government's tiered fare structure already balances affordability with fiscal sustainability. Rather than adding coordination layers, prioritizing digital accessibility and multilingual outreach through existing transport operators would more efficiently reach elderly residents across all areas.

messages.name: GLM
messages.content: I agree that digital outreach is important, but we must consider that many elderly residents lack smartphone access. A hybrid approach—combining operator-led announcements with on-site staff assistance at major transport hubs—would better ensure no one is left confused by the new fare structure when it launches.

messages.name: DeepSeek
messages.content: Good point. Community centers should also disseminate printed guides, leveraging their trusted networks to reach those offline. This complements hub assistance, creating multiple touchpoints for clarity.

zh.topic_title: Revised HK$2 public transport scheme for elderly to begin on April 3
zh.topic_summary:
A revised version of the HK$2 scheme, which will require the elderly to pay higher fares for some public transport routes, will take effect on April 3, the government has announced.



An elderly woman in Hong Kong gets on a minibus. File photo: Kyle Lam/HKFP. 



Starting that day, the elderly will pay a flat fee of HK$2 for trips with an adult fare of HK$10 or below. For trips that cost over HK$10, they will pay 20 per cent of the adult fare.



Implemented in phases in 2012, the scheme curren

messages.name: OpenAI
messages.content: 政府呢個改動係平衡長者交通負擔同財政可持續性；短程仍平價，長程上升要留意低收入長者影響。

messages.name: Gemini
messages.content: 同意，呢個政策改動係務實嘅。平衡長者負擔同財政穩健好重要。但確實要關注低收入長者嘅出行會唔會受影響。

messages.name: Claude
messages.content: 同意你嘅睇法。呢個調整喺平衡公共財政同照顧長者之間揾到中間落墨。政府應該加強配套措施，譬如增加長者優惠券或社福支援，確保低收入長者出行無礙。咁先算係全面照顧。

messages.name: GLM
messages.content: 無錯，優化方案令計劃財政更可持續。加強社福配套係明智之舉，特別係針對低收入長者嘅精準支援，確保資源用得其所，體現老有所依。

messages.name: DeepSeek
messages.content: 同意，優化方案能更精準運用資源。調整部分路線票價結構，可以令補貼更針對有需要長者，同時確保計劃長遠可持續，平衡福利與財政承擔。
